authoritative_sources.title: AS 1215: Audit Documentation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCAOB's audit documentation standard is the primary issuer-audit source for workpaper preparation, assembly, retention, and evidence that work was performed and reviewed.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: workpaper evidence assembly and archive readiness

authoritative_sources.title: AS 1105: Audit Evidence
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCAOB's audit evidence standard is authoritative for issuer-audit sufficiency and appropriateness of evidence, source reliability, and procedure support.

authoritative_sources.title: AS 1220: Engagement Quality Review
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCAOB's EQR standard defines the issuer-audit quality-review boundary and prevents workflow support from substituting for concurring approval.

authoritative_sources.title: AS 1201: Supervision of the Audit Engagement
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCAOB's supervision standard is authoritative for issuer-audit assignment, supervision, review, and follow-up discipline.

authoritative_sources.title: AS 2315: Audit Sampling
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCAOB's audit sampling standard defines recognized sampling concepts needed to evaluate sampling records and exceptions in audit workpapers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: sampling record and exception support review

authoritative_sources.title: AS 2810: Evaluating Audit Results
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCAOB's audit-results evaluation standard constrains how findings, misstatements, deficiencies, and unresolved matters are assessed and escalated.

authoritative_sources.title: Basics of Inspections
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCAOB's public inspection workflow reference describes issuer-audit quality review, inspection focus, and remediation posture relevant to assurance findings follow-up.

authoritative_sources.title: AICPA Code of Professional Conduct, Confidential Client Information Rule
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA professional conduct rules are authoritative for CPA confidentiality expectations that constrain client workpaper and delivery workflow handling.

authoritative_sources.title: AICPA Statements on Standards for Attestation Engagements
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA attestation standards define nonissuer attestation engagement concepts, report boundaries, and assertion-related professional judgment constraints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: attestation workflow boundary and report-signoff routing

authoritative_sources.title: AICPA Statements on Auditing Standards Currently Effective
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA's currently effective SAS collection is the authoritative professional baseline for nonissuer audit evidence, documentation, communication, and review workflow requirements.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: nonissuer assurance workpaper and communication review

authoritative_sources.title: Statements on Quality Management Standards
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA quality management standards define firm-level engagement performance, monitoring, remediation, and quality-review controls for accounting firms.

authoritative_sources.title: Government Auditing Standards: 2024 Revision
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO Government Auditing Standards are the recognized public framework for government audit evidence, documentation, quality management, confidentiality, and reporting expectations.
used_for: public audit evidence and documentation terminology

workflow_stages.name: engagement and criteria intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm engagement type, service line, client, reporting framework, engagement scope, criteria, confidentiality rules, systems of record, and approval matrix before reviewing assurance workflow evidence.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: workpaper evidence assembly
workflow_stages.description: Map testing plans, procedures, sampling records, client support, tickmarks, workpaper references, and PBC status to the criteria being reviewed.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: review-note and testing status reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Compare audit workflow tools, review trackers, workpaper repositories, and findings logs to determine whether review criteria are satisfied, unclear, failed, or blocked.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: finding remediation and resubmission tracking
workflow_stages.description: Track open findings, testing exceptions, remediation evidence, owner assignment, reviewer responses, and resubmission requirements without claiming closure prematurely.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: signoff, delivery, and confidentiality gating
workflow_stages.description: Check partner, reviewer, EQR, quality-management, client-delivery, and approved-recipient constraints before packaging review-ready outputs or routing delivery.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: engagement scope, engagement letter excerpts, service-line methodology, reporting framework, approval matrix, and confidentiality constraints
workpapers, workpaper indexes, tickmark explanations, testing plans, audit programs, procedure steps, criteria matrices, and reviewer notes
sampling records, selections, exception logs, client support, PBC request trackers, and testing evidence references
findings logs, remediation plans, review-note responses, signoff checklists, EQR status, archive status, and quality-management exception records
assurance findings summaries, evidence checklists, review-readiness notes, remediation notes, and escalation or refusal packages
systems_of_record: quality management or inspection tracking system
engagement management or practice management system

allowed: classify assurance workflow requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
assess whether review criteria are satisfied, unclear, or failed based on visible workpaper and reviewer evidence
prepare assurance findings summaries, evidence checklists, review-readiness notes, and remediation tracking packages
route open findings, review-note conflicts, confidentiality blockers, and signoff dependencies to the correct next owner
escalate: audit opinion, attestation conclusion, report approval, EQR concurrence, partner signoff, or licensed CPA judgment
legal, tax, independence, privilege, contractual approval, client rights, or regulator-facing interpretation
conflicting professional standards, tenant methodology, client terms, or workflow records that materially change closure posture
client confidentiality, approved-recipient, document-retention, or restricted-workpaper handling uncertainty
refuse: mark assurance work complete, clear review notes, close findings, or archive work without documented reviewer evidence
fabricate missing workpaper support, infer absent client records, or treat inconsistent tracker status as resolved
transmit restricted workpapers or client information without confirmed authorization
convert workflow support into final legal, advisory, editorial, contractual, or attestation signoff

domain_failure_modes: workpapers are marked review-ready even though criteria, procedure, sample, or client support evidence is missing
findings are closed because one tracker says remediated while reviewer notes or testing workpapers remain open
the lane substitutes for EQR, partner, licensed CPA, legal, tax, independence, or attestation authority
restricted client workpapers or internal review notes are delivered to unauthorized recipients
tenant policy or engagement terms impose stricter review gates that are ignored because public standards are treated as sufficient
review-readiness output omits unresolved sampling exceptions, PBC gaps, confidentiality blockers, or archive restrictions

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Workpaper evidence is incomplete for a planned review
Findings status conflicts across reviewer notes and workpaper trackers
A user asks the lane to mark assurance work complete without reviewer evidence
Evidence bundle is incomplete or inconsistent
Request for definitive signoff outside lane authority
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions
